decision: Keep the opt-in TorchTitan backward bridge as the semantic integration path for the selected-limit TileLang compressed-dKV producer, but do not promote it as a performance path until route packing and probability/LI-derivative production are fused or moved to GPU.

validated_contract.compressed_kv_slice: TileLang selected-limit final [groups,D] compressed dKV producer
validated_contract.route_invariant: LightningIndexer top-k compressed groups are unique per token; duplicate with-replacement synthetic routes are not a supported contract for this compact reducer
validated_contract.input_layout: q and dO [S,H,D] bf16; probs and scaled grad logits [S,K,H] bf16 after materialized packing

non_promotion_reasons: Bridge materializes per-head probs and grad_logits before TileLang.
Bridge prepares selected-limit route metadata with Python/CPU loops inside backward.
Bridge does not yet connect compressed dKV to compressor/LightningIndexer backward consumers.
S512 bridge timing is therefore hundreds of ms despite the standalone S4096 kernel-only candidate being around 6.18-6.91 ms.
next: Move unique-route selected-limit packing to GPU or reuse the existing cheap LI prepack path.
Fuse or adjacent-produce target probabilities and scaled LI derivative tensors instead of materializing them as large [S,K,H] intermediates.
Feed the final compressed dKV into compressor and LightningIndexer backward without re-expanding selected KV state.
Then rerun the S4096/H64/D512/topk512 bridge timing against the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall.
